Okay, so here's this concept of dissension kind of mapped out. So you get 100 people to buy a $100 thing, you get 20 people to buy a $2000 thing, 4 to buy a $6000 thing. From the second two rungs of this ladder, this value ladder, you're generating 64 out of the $70,000 of revenue. Alright, which means that if you were just offering this first thing, you'd only be able to spend $100 per lead, okay? Whereas at the very end of the phone, you can spend up to $740 per lead. Alright, sorry, per sale, excuse me. Does that, like, that should really, really sink, like, let that sink in because not offering these two rungs, which is what most people don't do, is why you can't afford to buy or purchase more leads and purchase more customers, right? And so to illustrate this from a cash flow standpoint, right, let's say you're spending, like, brain farting, you know what I want to say.
